TextENGNew: At first glance, a piece with no apparent connection to the world surrounding Rika Furude — one that seems to hold only the feelings of certain other girls.
But it is something precious — essential for grasping a truth that no one knew, a truth whose importance no one had yet perceived.
changeReason: New translation distinguishes two separate ideas from the Japanese: a truth no one knew, and a truth whose importance no one perceived — the old version collapses these into one clause.

TextENGNew: The year 1982 — one year before the tragedy.

It was the time when Satoshi had vanished, and Rika and the others were agonizing over how to escape the dead end bearing down on them in the year to come — when a certain female detective paid a visit to Hinamizawa.
changeReason: "the Rikas" (implying multiple versions of Rika) vs "Rika and the others" (Rika and her companions); 梨花たち means "Rika and the others," not multiple Rikas.

TextENGNew: And the one who drew that detective's attention was... one of the club members, Rena Ryuugu.
It was the assault incident that had occurred when she was still living in Ibaraki.
That fact was unknown to most of the club members — even Keiichi did not fully understand its cause.
changeReason: "didn't know the exact cause" implies complete ignorance, while "did not fully understand its cause" implies partial knowledge; Japanese 正確に理解していなかった means "did not accurately understand."

TextENGNew: And yet... there were those red capsules that Rena had once been taking.
changeReason: "pills" vs "capsules" — the Japanese カプセル specifically means capsules, making the new translation more accurate.

TextENGNew: Those pills were born of a conspiracy that neither Rika nor even Takano had perceived — and Rena had been its victim...
To expose that truth, one detective — Tomoe Minai — rose up and overcame every obstacle in her path.
In response to that — the light of hope, which until now had burned so faintly, blazed brighter and stronger until it could be clearly seen.
And in that light... something came into view — something whose very existence even I had not known of...
